Operation 'retryWebhookEvent' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description lacks specificity about inputs (path parameters), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (retry count updates, timestamp changes). This improves AI agent ability to determine when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Retries sending a failed webhook event. This will attempt to deliver the webhook payload again to the configured endpoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Retry delivery of a failed webhook event
data.description_suggestion: Retries delivery of a previously failed webhook event to its configured endpoint. Requires the webhook ID and event ID as path parameters. The operation will attempt to resend the original webhook payload. Returns the updated event object with new delivery attempt status and timestamp. Side effects include updating the event's retry count and last attempted delivery time.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks specificity about inputs (path parameters), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (retry count updates, timestamp changes). Enhanced version clarifies that webhookId and eventId are required path parameters, specifies the return value structure, and documents state changes that occur during retry. This improves AI agent ability to determine when to use this operation and what to expect.
